refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use, ensuring data acquisition, processing, and dissemination meet the latency target.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition, processing, and dissemination, which are the key contributors to latency.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data capture to worksite model update availability under representative operational scenarios, verifying p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time worksite data with minimal delay
FR2: Process and update the worksite model efficiently
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules within latency constraints

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with prioritized network scheduling
DP2: Implement incremental model update algorithms optimized for low-latency processing
DP3: Employ multicast dissemination over dedicated network links to subscribed modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address acquisition, processing, and dissemination respectively

potential_sources: Tight coupling between data acquisition and processing timing
Dependency of dissemination on processing completion
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with well-defined interfaces to maintain low coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports prioritized telemetry and multicast
Risk of processing delays if model update algorithms are not optimized
Risk of interface bottlenecks if dissemination is not properly managed
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor processing algorithm performance and network capabilities; consider fallback mechanisms if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for data acquisition, processing, and dissemination of worksite model updates

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and vehicle data streams for acquisition

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Disseminates updated worksite model to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Coordinates update requests and acknowledgments with Vehicle and Teleoperation

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary containment of update logic and data flows; low diffusion due to centralized update management
Receives updates but does not perform core update processing
Consumes updates for operator interface; no update processing
Minimal involvement in update latency function

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing update latency functions within NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports latency targets.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Interface latency with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules could impact overall latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate load balancing and interface optimization within NGHE Solution; consider partial delegation if bottlenecks arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well decomposed and the selected DPs logically address each sub-FR with clear mapping.

diffusion_risks: Centralization in NGHE Solution may cause diffusion if interfaces are not tightly controlled
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the primary module; interfaces are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end within NGHE Solution using telemetry and network link monitoring.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of sub-functions to NGHE Solution with interfaces to Vehicle and Teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Monitor interface latency impact on overall update latency
Consider fallback or distributed processing if NGHE Solution module load is high

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time worksite data with minimal delay
FR2: Process and update the worksite model efficiently
FR3: Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules within latency constraints

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports prioritized telemetry and multicast
Processing algorithms can meet latency targets
open_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution module
